rationale: The two papers are tightly aligned on sales force compensation design: the citing study tests cash vs. noncash incentives in a field intervention, while the cited study models and empirically tests incentive intensity and selection roles in sales compensation contracts. The citing note explicitly frames its contribution against standard principal-agent expectations, which the cited paper directly addresses through an agency-based compensation framework. Given this strong conceptual overlap and plausible foundational/theory use, this is not an obvious incidental mention.
